depression.topic: Feelings of inadequacy after unproductive weeks
depression.description: I often feel like a pathetic person when I have an unproductive week. It's hard to shake the thought that I'm never good at planning or organizing my time, which just adds to my feelings of failure.

depression.topic: Struggles with financial dependency on parents
depression.description: Relying on my parents for financial support makes me feel incompetent and worthless. I can't help but think that I should be able to take care of myself, but instead, I'm just a burden.

depression.topic: Bitterness towards education and past experiences
depression.description: I feel a deep bitterness towards my college experience at Grinnell. I was so isolated and disconnected that it makes me resent education, and I wonder if I’ll ever want to return.
The expectations from my mother weigh heavily on me. I feel like I must meet her standards, or else I'm just a failure in her eyes, which makes it hard to even think about my future.
I tend to isolate myself when I feel overwhelmed by feelings of inadequacy. It's like I believe that if I just work hard alone, I can avoid confronting my failures.

Delusion.topic: Perceived failure in personal and academic life
Delusion.description: I constantly feel like I'm failing at everything I do, especially when it comes to my education. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of believing I’m worthless and will never achieve anything.
I have this overwhelming fear of disappointing my parents. Every time I think about my past failures, I can hear their disappointment echoing in my mind, which makes me feel even worse about myself.
